text: The hall at Tara is quiet now, and I am the reason for the quiet. Murchadh mac Fáelán did not need to raise his voice — the board said everything he wished to say, and the board said it plainly.
I walk out of Tara with the clothes on my back and the game in my head. There are roads west — there are always roads west — and people who have not yet heard my name said wrong.

Connacht. The Atlantic comes in hard off the headlands and everything here smells of salt and old rain. These people have their own games, their own grudges. I am nobody here, and that is where I begin.

Fiachra watched me go without a word, and I felt his eyes on my back for a long while. The road east is longer than the road I came by. Murchadh is at the end of it, and he does not know I am walking.

speaker: Murchadh mac Fáelán
text: I have heard your name. I expected something more.

speaker: Murchadh mac Fáelán
text: Go west. There may be people there who will play you.

speaker: Séanán na Farraige
text: Sit, sit — I was going to mend the net but it can wait. You look like someone who has been walking a while.

speaker: Séanán na Farraige
text: Ha. Well. The fish will not mock me for it, I suppose. You play like someone who has lost before and did not forget it.

speaker: Séanán na Farraige
text: There it is. I do not always see the whole board but sometimes the board shows me anyway. Stay and eat, if you like.

speaker: Séanán na Farraige
text: Back already. I thought you might be. Here — the board is where you left it.

speaker: Brigid na Scailpe
text: I do not play in haste and I do not play in company I do not trust. Sit with me a while first. Then we will see.

speaker: Brigid na Scailpe
text: You protect the King as though you have been the King yourself once. That is not a common thing.

speaker: Brigid na Scailpe
text: You opened too early. Patience is not the absence of movement — it is knowing which movement to withhold.

speaker: Brigid na Scailpe
text: You have thought about it since. I can see that. Good — thinking is most of the game.

speaker: Tadhg an Ósta
text: I have beaten three men today already and the night is young. Set the board — I do not have all evening.

speaker: Tadhg an Ósta
text: Right, right — fair enough. Who sent you? Séanán? He has the look of someone who teaches quietly.

speaker: Tadhg an Ósta
text: You held too long — a man who will not press his advantage is a man asking to lose. Come back when you have the stomach for it.

speaker: Tadhg an Ósta
text: Again. Fine. This time I will not make it easy.

speaker: Fiachra an Fhánaí
text: I have heard three different things about you from three different people, and none of them agreed. Sit — I want to see which one is true.

speaker: Fiachra an Fhánaí
text: There is someone east of here who has not seen that. I wonder if you will get there before he hears of it.

speaker: Fiachra an Fhánaí
text: You protect the centre well enough. But you read the board — you do not read the player. That is the gap.

speaker: Fiachra an Fhánaí
text: You came back. That is the first thing I needed to know about you.

speaker: Séanán na Farraige
text: You came back quieter than you left, and you played quieter too. That is how the sea teaches — not with words.

speaker: Brigid na Scailpe
text: You held the thing you learned and did not let the losing spoil it. That is the harder discipline, and you had it.

speaker: Tadhg an Ósta
text: All right, all right — you came back and you came back better, I will give you that much and not a word more.

speaker: Fiachra an Fhánaí
text: The second thing I needed to know about you — you came back and you won. The road east is yours.
